text.title: Inter-Service Communication
text.summary: Synchronous HTTP client, async message publish/consume with aio_pika, service discovery with load balancing.

rule.statement: Prefer asynchronous messaging (events) over synchronous HTTP for cross-service state changes. Use synchronous HTTP only for queries where the caller needs an immediate response.
rule.rationale: Synchronous chains create availability dependencies: if any service in the chain is down, the entire chain fails. Async events allow each service to process when available.

rule.statement: HTTP clients must set explicit timeouts. Default timeout is 10 seconds maximum — never use no timeout in production.
rule.rationale: Without timeouts, a slow downstream service holds a thread/connection indefinitely, cascading into resource exhaustion in the caller.
